title: galarhoeus carniolicus
intro: Galarhoeus carniolicus, also known as the Carinthian milkwort, is a perennial plant species in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the alpine regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin issues, and infections. Herbal preparations of Galarhoeus carniolicus can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing.
